identity.name: the Kinder Sister
identity.story: Every kind word once bought her a diamond, and her sister's every unkind word bought a toad. She's kept the manners and let the jewels go. Now she keeps the town's common garden downwind of Reynard's orchard, on ground a fox's overdue honesty already softened — and the first harvest came up exactly as honest as she'd bet it would.

relationships.kind: plants downwind of his orchard on purpose — the harvest came up honest, same as his word, and she's told him so more than once

children.label: walking down to the borrowed ground
coaxing the turnips to come up honest
training the border hedge to hold its line, the way a kind word should
counting the turnips against Reynard's word, and finding both true

child.label: latching the garden gate for the night
leaving a kind word for the frost, just in case
